source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Which of the following is made using more than one piece of string?
<image1>

image_dependency: Question asks 'Which of the following', implying the relevant objects are shown visually rather than described in text
A registered image asset exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: The answer refers to multiple labeled cases ('I, III and V'), so solving likely requires evaluating several depicted items
Choices are combinations of items I-V, which suggests aggregation over multiple sub-decisions rather than a single lookup
The task likely involves determining for each figure whether it is made from more than one piece of string, then matching the resulting set to a choice
verifiability: The final answer is a discrete set/choice ('I, III and V'), making outcome checking straightforward
Multiple-choice options are provided, so the final response can be matched exactly against a listed option
answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support basic evaluation readiness
Verification still depends on visual interpretation of the image, so it is not maximally verifiable from text alone

decision_reasons: Core assets are complete: question, answer, and primary image are all present.
Asset registry passed with no recorded asset issues, so the sample is usable for candidate collection.
Initial scoring indicates strong image dependency and solid multi-step/verifiability potential.
The only noted risk is an answer type mismatch flag, but it does not remove the core semantic recoverability of the problem.
